channel.title: Neurodivergence on Kelly and Coffee
channel.description: Recent content in Neurodivergence on Kelly and Coffee

item.title: A Second Read: Using AI When Rejection-Sensitive Dysphoria Hits
item.description: If you’ve read my post on RSD at work, you already know the shape of this one. Rejection-sensitive dysphoria is the part of my ADHD that turns a neutral sentence into a knife. Someone writes “can we talk later?” and my body reacts as though I’ve already been fired, dumped, and exiled, all before lunch. The feeling is instant, physical, and completely out of proportion — and knowing that intellectually does almost nothing to stop it in the moment.

item.title: Window of tolerance
item.description: I have a therapist. Yes. Why? Oh, I could list a number of reasons, but suffice to say it is to better myself. I have some difficulties coping with extreme emotions, or at least emotions I perceive to be extreme. In discussing this, my therapist wanted to explain something to me. So she drew “the window of tolerance”. For some reason, this seemed so logical, and yet I had never realized this. It explained so much.
